_comment_3: Substitute {{PLUGIN_HOOKS_DIR}} with the resolved plugin hooks directory (default: ~/.claude/plugins/idea-to-deploy/hooks). Legacy sync-to-active.sh installs use ~/.claude/hooks.
_comment_4: If the user already has .claude/settings.json, /adopt merges hooks entries (by command path) AND permissions.ask entries (by rule string) without duplicating. User's existing permissions.allow / permissions.deny / other permissions.ask rules, env, statusLine, and model are preserved — never removed.
_comment_5: Recommended ASK guardrails for dangerous OS tool-classes via NATIVE Claude Code permissions (no custom DSL — omnigent OS-tool-class ASK ported onto the harness's own allow/deny/ask). They prompt for confirmation before genuinely destructive shell commands. Rule form Bash(cmd:*) matches when the FIRST token equals cmd, so Bash(sudo:*) also catches sudo-escalated variants. Egress (curl/wget) is intentionally left to pii-egress-guard.sh, and destructive git/db to careful.sh, to avoid double-prompting. Bash(chmod:*) is intentionally OMITTED (chmod +x fires constantly in normal dev — add it back for locked-down projects). kill/killall/pkill prompt frequently in dev-server workflows; tune or remove per project. Note: command-name rules do not catch full-path (/bin/rm) or indirect (find -delete) variants — careful.sh covers the destructive-pattern angle.
